meta_title: TripFlow · AI travel planner (Bachelor's Thesis) | Diego Sánchez Rincón
meta_description: TripFlow: an AI-assisted travel planner with real-time collaboration, interactive maps and offline access. Spring Boot microservices backend with a React PWA frontend. Bachelor's Thesis, graded 10/10.
tagline: An AI-assisted travel planner that brings the whole trip together in one place, with real-time collaboration, interactive maps and full offline access.
image_alt: Two smartphones showing the TripFlow app: a dashboard with trip stats and a trip detail screen for a Japan itinerary.
problem_lede: Planning a trip means juggling half a dozen tools that don't talk to each other. The result is information that's scattered, copied by hand and out of date almost immediately.

problem_points.text: Information is scattered across search engines, maps, blogs and chats.
Nothing is connected: every detail is copied by hand and goes stale instantly.
In a group it multiplies: different preferences and changes that get lost.

compare_heading: The whole trip in one place
compare_intro: Most tools solve only a slice of the problem. TripFlow set out to cover the whole of it (time-based planning, AI, real-time collaboration and offline) in a single product.
features_intro: Everything you need to plan a trip, from the first idea to a shareable, offline-ready itinerary.

features.title: Day-by-day itineraries
features.desc: Plan trips by days and activities, with optimized routes between places.

features.title: AI assistant
features.desc: Generate a complete itinerary from a single prompt with an AI assistant.

features.title: Real-time collaboration
features.desc: Plan together with live, role-based collaboration.

features.title: Interactive map + PDF
features.desc: Visualize everything on an interactive map and export a clean PDF.

features.title: Public share links
features.desc: Share any itinerary through a secure public link.

features.title: Cross-platform PWA · Offline
features.desc: An installable PWA that works on any device, even with no connection.

arch_intro: TripFlow is a distributed system: a React PWA talks to a set of Spring Boot microservices through a gateway, and those services coordinate asynchronously over an Apache Kafka event bus.
decisions_intro: A handful of decisions shaped the project the most, around security, AI, real-time and offline.

decisions.title: JWT authentication
decisions.desc: Dual JWT (15-min access, 30-day refresh) in HttpOnly cookies to mitigate XSS.

decisions.title: Async, secure AI
decisions.desc: Prompt-injection filtering on the way in, and sanitization of the generated JSON on the way out.

decisions.title: Real time
decisions.desc: WebSocket + STOMP with JWT validation in the handshake itself to authorize events.

decisions.title: Offline PWA
decisions.desc: A service worker installs the app and lets you consult itineraries with no connection, mid-trip.

metrics_intro: Built with a strong testing and automation culture from day one: every change ships through the same pipeline.
journey_intro: TripFlow was my Bachelor's Thesis at Universidad Rey Juan Carlos, graded 10/10, designed and built end to end. It grew iteratively across three versions, and each one shipped as a working, deployed release rather than a prototype, from the first commit to v1.0.0.

milestones.name: Product
milestones.desc: Maps, collaboration, share links, PWA and PDF.
